working_definition: CD4+ T helper cell present in adult human skin. Identified in Reynolds et al. (2021) as part of the T cell compartment in adult skin. Includes Th1, Th2, and Th17 subsets, characterised by CD4, CD40LG, IL4, IL13, IL17A, IFNG expression.

best_match.cl_definition: A mature T cell that differentiates into one of several T helper cell subtypes (Th1, Th2, Th17, etc.) and assists other lymphocytes by releasing cytokines. Typically expresses CD4.

justification: CL:0000912 'helper T cell' directly corresponds to the Th population in Reynolds et al. (2021), which encompasses CD4+ T helper cells with Th1, Th2, and Th17 polarisation profiles in adult skin.
recommendation: Use CL:0000912 (helper T cell) as an exact match for the Th cell type.
